MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Nov-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendants Brendan Quinlan, an individual, and Brendan Quinlan, an individual dba Quinlan Construction's motion to set aside the defaults entered against them on August 23, 2019, is granted. Defendants' showing supports the conclusion that the defaults were taken as a result of mistake, surprise, inadvertence, or excusable neglect because Mr. Quinlan, a non-lawyer who had never done any business with Liberty Mutual Insurance (which is suing as a subrogee), did not understand the summons or why he was being sued. (Quinlan Decl. 2.) "Because the law strongly favors trial and disposition on the merits, any doubts in applying section 473 must be resolved in favor of the party seeking relief from default." (Elston v. City of Turlock (1985) 38 Cal.3d 227, 233.) Moreover, where, as here, the party in default moves promptly to seek relief, and no prejudice to the opposing party will result from setting aside the default and letting the case go to trial on the merits, "very slight evidence will be required to justify a court in setting aside the default." (Id.) Defendants shall remit $1,000 in fees to plaintiff as reasonable fees for obtaining the default. (See Code Civ. Proc., 473(c)(1)(C); Rogalski v. Nabers Cadillac (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 816, 823.) Defendants shall file their answer within 10 days.

The Court signed the proposed order submitted by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-07-2019 Continued To Feb-26-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
